name: Vector3D
javadoc:
/** * This class implements vectors in a three-dimensional space. *

Instance of this class are guaranteed to be immutable.

* @since 1.2 */

members.name: Vector3D
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Simple constructor.
 * Build a vector from its azimuthal coordinates
 * @param alpha azimuth (α) around Z
 *              (0 is +X, π/2 is +Y, π is -X and 3π/2 is -Y)
 * @param delta elevation (δ) above (XY) plane, from -π/2 to +π/2
 * @see #getAlpha()
 * @see #getDelta()
 */

tags.content:
azimuth (α) around Z

members.name: Vector3D
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Multiplicative constructor
 * Build a vector from another one and a scale factor.
 * The vector built will be a * u
 * @param a scale factor
 * @param u base (unscaled) vector
 */

members.name: Vector3D
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Linear constructor
 * Build a vector from two other ones and corresponding scale factors.
 * The vector built will be a1 * u1 + a2 * u2
 * @param a1 first scale factor
 * @param u1 first base (unscaled) vector
 * @param a2 second scale factor
 * @param u2 second base (unscaled) vector
 */

members.name: Vector3D
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Linear constructor
 * Build a vector from three other ones and corresponding scale factors.
 * The vector built will be a1 * u1 + a2 * u2 + a3 * u3
 * @param a1 first scale factor
 * @param u1 first base (unscaled) vector
 * @param a2 second scale factor
 * @param u2 second base (unscaled) vector
 * @param a3 third scale factor
 * @param u3 third base (unscaled) vector
 */

members.name: Vector3D
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Linear constructor
 * Build a vector from four other ones and corresponding scale factors.
 * The vector built will be a1 * u1 + a2 * u2 + a3 * u3 + a4 * u4
 * @param a1 first scale factor
 * @param u1 first base (unscaled) vector
 * @param a2 second scale factor
 * @param u2 second base (unscaled) vector
 * @param a3 third scale factor
 * @param u3 third base (unscaled) vector
 * @param a4 fourth scale factor
 * @param u4 fourth base (unscaled) vector
 */

members.javadoc:
/** * Get a vector orthogonal to the instance. *

There are an infinite number of normalized vectors orthogonal * to the instance. This method picks up one of them almost * arbitrarily. It is useful when one needs to compute a reference * frame with one of the axes in a predefined direction. The * following example shows how to build a frame having the k axis * aligned with the known vector u : * 


 *   Vector3D k = u.normalize();
 *   Vector3D i = k.orthogonal();
 *   Vector3D j = Vector3D.crossProduct(k, i);
 *

* @return a new normalized vector orthogonal to the instance * @exception MathArithmeticException if the norm of the instance is null */

tags.content: new normalized vector orthogonal to the instance
tags.description: a new normalized vector orthogonal to the instance

tags.name: MathArithmeticException
tags.content: if the norm of the instance is null
tags.description: if the norm of the instance is null

members.name: angle
members.javadoc:
/** * Compute the angular separation between two vectors. *

This method computes the angular separation between two * vectors using the dot product for well separated vectors and the * cross product for almost aligned vectors. This allows to have a * good accuracy in all cases, even for vectors very close to each * other.

* @param v1 first vector * @param v2 second vector * @return angular separation between v1 and v2 * @exception MathArithmeticException if either vector has a null norm */

tags.description: angular separation between v1 and v2

tags.name: MathArithmeticException
tags.content: if either vector has a null norm
tags.description: if either vector has a null norm

equals
members.javadoc:
/** * Test for the equality of two 3D vectors. *

* If all coordinates of two 3D vectors are exactly the same, and none are * Double.NaN, the two 3D vectors are considered to be equal. *

*

* NaN coordinates are considered to affect globally the vector * and be equals to each other - i.e, if either (or all) coordinates of the * 3D vector are equal to Double.NaN, the 3D vector is equal to * {@link #NaN}. *

* * @param other Object to test for equality to this * @return true if two 3D vector objects are equal, false if * object is null, not an instance of Vector3D, or * not equal to this Vector3D instance */

tags.name: other
tags.content: Object to test for equality to this
tags.description: Object to test for equality to this

tags.content: if two 3D vector objects are equal, false if object is null, not an instance of Vector3D, or not equal to this Vector3D instance
tags.description: true if two 3D vector objects are equal, false if object is null, not an instance of Vector3D, or not equal to this Vector3D instance

* @see MathArrays#linearCombination(double, double, double, double, double, double) */

members.name: distance1
members.javadoc:
/** * Compute the distance between two vectors according to the L1 norm. *

Calling this method is equivalent to calling: * v1.subtract(v2).getNorm1() except that no intermediate * vector is built

* @param v1 first vector * @param v2 second vector * @return the distance between v1 and v2 according to the L1 norm */

tags.content: distance between v1 and v2 according to the L<sub>1</sub> norm
tags.description: the distance between v1 and v2 according to the L<sub>1</sub> norm

members.name: distance
members.javadoc:
/** * Compute the distance between two vectors according to the L2 norm. *

Calling this method is equivalent to calling: * v1.subtract(v2).getNorm() except that no intermediate * vector is built

* @param v1 first vector * @param v2 second vector * @return the distance between v1 and v2 according to the L2 norm */

tags.content: distance between v1 and v2 according to the L<sub>2</sub> norm
tags.description: the distance between v1 and v2 according to the L<sub>2</sub> norm

Calling this method is equivalent to calling: * v1.subtract(v2).getNormInf() except that no intermediate * vector is built

* @param v1 first vector * @param v2 second vector * @return the distance between v1 and v2 according to the L∞ norm */
